original: Mongol army takes the Jin city of Caizhou, last holdout of Jin Emperor Ai-Tsung, who commits suicide rather than be captured
Synod of Breslau orders Jews of Silesia to wear special cone shaped hats as a means of identification, the traditional garb had become less prevalent
Massive storm across south-east England hits Cinque Ports realines the coastline, New Romney port now a mile inland
France & Venice sign treaty against Milan
Pope Paul III routes Cardinal Pole to England
The first recorded horse race meeting in England is held at Roodee Fields in Chester
Battle at London: Sir Thomas Wyatt defeated and his rebellion against Queen Mary crushed
Bishop of Gloucester John Hooper is burned at the stake for heresy
Alexander Ludovisi is elected Pope Gregory XV (-1623)
Treaty of Andrussovo: Russia and Poland signs peace treaty
English reconquer NY from Netherlands
Thomas Otway's "Venice Preserved" premieres in London
Battle at Toulon (French/Spanish vs English fleet of Adm Matthews)
British Parliament declares Massachusetts Colony in rebellion
Austria declares war on Russia
USS Constellation captures French frigate Insurgents off Nevis, W I
France & Austria sign Peace of Luneville
American Indian Society organizes
Haiti invades the newly founded Dominican Republic.
Roman Republic declared in Rome replacing following flight of Pope Pius IX
Tennessee votes against secession (US Civil War)
Federal fish protection office authorized by US Congress
1st Japanese arrive in Hawaii
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) is established as a Cabinet-level agency
1st shipment of asparagus arrives in San Francisco from Sacramento
Canal builder De Lesseps & others sentenced to prison for fraud
William Morgan presents his new sport "Mintonette" to the world at Springfield College, Massachusetts, later renames it "Volleyball"
Natal proclaims state of siege in Zulu uprising
1st US federal legislation on narcotics prohibits importation, possession, and use of "smoking opium"
US Tennis Association amends rule taking bye away from defending champion
Beginning of the Ten Tragic Days in Mexico City, with revolts leading to 3,000 deaths
Britain's military service act enforced (conscription)
NL votes down a proposal by Giants, Braves, & Cubs to increase club player limit from 21 to 22 (The Reds want to decrease to 20)
Sacha Guitry's "Deburan" premieres in Paris
US Army chaplain school organizes at Ft Monroe, Virginia
International treaty recognizes Norwegian sovereignty over Svalbard
Joint Rules Com bans foreign substances & alterations to baseballs
Brazil becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty
Italian government of Bonomi falls
Snow on Mauna Loa, Hawaii
World War Foreign Debt Commission is established by Congress to settle the problem of Allied war and postwar loans
Soviet Union Council of Labour and Defence passes resolution creating the  Civil Air Fleet of the USSR, the forerunner of Aeroflot Airline
Nakhichevan ASSR constituted within Azerbaijan SSR
Haifa Technion (Israel) opens
Teaching theory of evolution forbidden in Atlanta, Georgia schools
USSR, Estonia, Latvia, Poland & Romania sign Litvinov's Pact, providing for renunciation of war among its signatories
US airship Columbia crashes during storm (Flushing, NY)
USA enters Olympic 2-man bobsled competition for 1st time
-63°F (-53°C), Moran, Wyoming (state record)
-14.3°F (-25.7°C), coldest day in New York City
-51°F (-46°C), Vanderbilt, Michigan (state record)
Balkan Entente alliance forms (Yugoslavia, Greece, Turkey & Romania)
German skier Franz Pfnür wins the inaugural Olympic men’s combined gold medal at the Garmisch-Partenkirchen Winter Games ahead of teammate Gustav Lantschner
Belgian Spaak government falls
British troops conquer El Agheila
Nazi collaborators destroy pro-Jewish café Alcazar Amsterdam (Alcazar refused to hang "No Entry for Jews" signs in front of cafe)
Daylight Savings War Time goes into effect in US
Japanese troops land near Makassar, South Celebes
Philadelphia "Phillies" change nickname (temporarily) to "Phils"
Japanese troops evacuate Guadalcanal, ending the epic World War II battle on the Solomon Islands in the Pacific
National League of Baseball seeks a buyer for the Philadelphia Phillies as owner Gerry Nugent falls in arrears
U-734/U-238 sunk off Ireland
-Feb 10] Germany destroys Ruhrdammen
The Battle of the Atlantic: HMS Venturer sinks U-Boat 864 off the coast of Norway
WAAF-corporal flies along the tail of a Spitfire
Dutch Labor Party (Dutch Social Democratic Party) forms
WLWT TV channel 5 in Cincinnati, OH (NBC) begins broadcasting
WNEP TV channel 16 in Scranton Wilkes-Barre, PA (ABC) 1st broadcast
Mario Scelba forms new government in Italy
Dutch 2nd Chamber accepts crematory law
Unity Committee of US trade unions American Federation of Labor (AFL) and Congress of Industrial Organizations (CIO) agree to merge, subject to ratification by each group's executive committee
-5°F (-20.6°C) in Sicily
KHPL (now KWNB) TV channel 6 in Hayes Center, NB (ABC) 1st broadcast
R Lacoste follows Catroux as premier of Algeria
AFL & NFL agree verbally to a no tampering pact
Joseph Ileo appointed Premier of Congo
The Beatles first gig at Liverpool's Cavern Club; they would play there nearly 300 times over the next two years
Jamaica signs agreement to become independent

1st test flight of Boeing 727 jet
7th largest snowfall in NYC history (42.4 cm, 16.7")

IX Winter Olympic Games close in Innsbruck, Austria
NYC news anchor Jim Jenson's 1st appearance on WCBS-TV

First gay-themed TV episode - All in the Family
Quake in San Fernando Valley kills 64 & causes over $½B damage
Satchel Paigebecomes 1st Negro League player elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
British government declares a state of emergency after a month-long coal miners' strike

"Daddy What If" by Bobby Bare peaks at #41
Soviet Soyuz 17 returns to Earth
Oscar Charleston selected to baseball's Hall of Fame
ABC airs "Heroes of Rock N Roll" special, featuring archival performances of 62 artists
New York City premiere of Paul Schrader's allegedly fact based film "Hardcore"
Nigeria amends constitution
UK Football club Nottingham Forest makes £1m transfer deal
Walter Hill's crime drama film "The Warriors",  based on Sol Yurick's 1965 novel, is released in the United States, sparking gang violence at many theaters and a halt to the film's marketing campaign
Belgium buys 44 F-16s

Halley's Comet reaches 30th perihelion (closest approach to Sun)
Haydar Bakr al-Attas appointed president of South Yemen

NY Stock Exch installs ladies restroom in the Exchange Luncheon Club
NH begins a NCAA record 32-game losing streak at home (ends Feb 1991)

Heike Henkel high jumps female indoor world record (2.07m)

WYNY-FM in NYC changes calls to WKTU-FM

American submarine USS Greeneville accidentally strikes and sinks the Ehime-Maru, a Japanese training vessel operated by the Uwajima Fishery High School; nine the Ehime-Maru's crew members were killed, including four high school students.
Latvia leaves deep recession with an annualised growth of 3.7% in the last quarter of 2010, it is reported today
5 people are killed after Cessna plane crashes on a Brussels runway
Australian National University scientists discover the oldest known star at 13.6 billion years old
Olympic teams figure skating event debuts in Sochi with Russia taking the gold medal; pairs champions Tatiana Volosozhar and Maxim Trankov win first of 2 gold at the Games
Protest erupt in Sarajevo and other cities in Bosnia-Herzegovina; the unemployment rate remains at 40% (57% for youth)

Switzerland votes to return immigration quotas
Two German passenger trains collide near Bad Aibling, Bavaria, killing 10 and seriously injuring 18
Deaths from COVID-19 virus overtake those of Sars (2003) with 813 deaths worldwide, with more than 34,800 known infections

Fastest conventional plane flight from New York to London made by British Airways Boeing 747-436 in 4 hours and 56 minutes; average speed of 1,327 km/h / 825 mph, aided by tailwinds from due to Storm Ciara
Solar Orbiter probe attached to an Atlas rocket lifts off from Cape Canaveral
Storm Ciara makes landfall in northern Scotland bringing widespread wind and flooding to northern Europe resulting in 18 deaths
Suspected militants attack and kill at least 30 people, many while sleeping in their cars in Auno, north-eastern Nigeria, kidnapping women and children
United Arab Emirates is the fifth entity to successfully orbit a probe around Mars, as probe Hope begins to study the planet's atmosphere.
WHO team investigating origin of COVID-19 in Wuhan say it is "extremely unlikely" that it leaked from a local research lab
Nearly four million bottles of beer destroyed in large crackdown on alcohol in northern Nigerian state of Kano, where alcohol prohibited under Sharia law

US PresidentUlysses S Grantsigns law resulting in US Army Signal Service’s establishment of its "Division of Telegrams and Reports for the Benefit of Commerce", later known as the National Weather Service

Beginning ofJames Clavell's novel "Whirlwind"
Rick Barryof the Houston Rockets is the 1st in NBA history to score eight 3-pointers in a game

Kendrick Lamarperforms halftime at Super Bowl LIX, with an appearance bySamuel L. Jackson

Confederate Provisional Congress declares all laws under the US Constitution were consistent with constitution of Confederate states (US Civil War)
Invasion of Benin City by British Expeditionary Force; the city is burned and looted, ending the Nigerian Kingdom of Benin formed in the 11th century

Japanese torpedo boats launch a surprise attack on Russian ships at the Port Arthur naval base in Manchuria, beginning the Russo-Japanese War. Japanese troops also land at Chemulpo (Incheon), near Seoul, Korea; in three weeks, they advance to the Yalu River, the border of Manchuria.
